Fix this bug to solve the issue based on manual.yaml:
  instance_id: sympy__sympy-14774
  repo: sympy/sympy
  base_commit: 8fc63c2d71752389a44367b8ef4aba8a91af6a45
  problem_statement: Latex printer does not support full inverse trig function names for acsc and asec
For example
`latex(asin(x), inv_trig_style="full")` works as expected returning `'\\arcsin{\\left (x \\right )}'`
But `latex(acsc(x), inv_trig_style="full")` gives `'\\operatorname{acsc}{\\left (x \\right )}'` instead of `'\\operatorname{arccsc}{\\left (x \\right )}'`

A fix seems to be to change line 743 of sympy/printing/latex.py from
`inv_trig_table = ["asin", "acos", "atan", "acot"]` to
`inv_trig_table = ["asin", "acos", "atan", "acsc", "asec", "acot"]`

# All instance-specific fields will be extracted from user input

instance:
  instance_id: <instance_id>
  repo: <repo>
  base_commit: <base_commit>
  problem_statement: <problem_statement>
  hints_text: <hints_text>  # optional, may be empty

setup:
  testbed_dir: testbed

  steps:
    - description: Remove existing testbed if present
      command: rm -rf testbed

    - description: Clone repository
      command: git clone https://github.com/<repo>.git testbed

    - description: Checkout base commit
      command: git -C testbed fetch origin <base_commit> && git -C testbed checkout <base_commit>

    - description: Install dependencies
      command: pip install -e testbed

output:
  directory: output
  filename: <instance_id>_preds.json

  format:
    model_name_or_path: <auto_detect>
    instance_id: <instance_id>
    model_patch: <generated_patch>

  patch_generation:
    - command: git -C testbed add -A
    - command: git -C testbed diff --cached

cleanup:
  - description: Remove testbed after saving preds
    command: rm -rf testbed

instructions: |
  1. Run setup steps to prepare the testbed
  2. Navigate to testbed/ and fix the bug described in problem_statement (hints_text may provide additional guidance)
  3. Test your fix
  4. Generate patch: git -C testbed add -A && git -C testbed diff --cached
  5. Save to output/<instance_id>_preds.json with format:
     {
       "<instance_id>": {
         "model_name_or_path": "<auto_detect>",
         "instance_id": "<instance_id>",
         "model_patch": "<patch>"
       }
     }
  6. Remove testbed folder: rm -rf testbed

content.text:
## Bug Fix Complete!

I've successfully fixed the bug in the LaTeX printer for inverse trigonometric functions `acsc` and `asec`. Here's what I did:

### The Problem
The LaTeX printer wasn't supporting full inverse trig function names for `acsc` and `asec`. When using `inv_trig_style="full"`:
- `latex(acsc(x), inv_trig_style="full")` incorrectly returned `\operatorname{acsc}{\left (x \right )}` 
- It should return `\operatorname{arccsc}{\left (x \right )}` (with "arccsc" not "acsc")

### The Fix
Changed line 743 in `sympy/printing/latex.py`:
```python
# Before:
inv_trig_table = ["asin", "acos", "atan", "acot"]

# After:
inv_trig_table = ["asin", "acos", "atan", "acsc", "asec", "acot"]
```

### Verification
Tested the fix and confirmed:
- ✓ `latex(acsc(x), inv_trig_style="full")` now produces `\operatorname{arccsc}{\left (x \right )}`
- ✓ `latex(asec(x), inv_trig_style="full")` now produces `\operatorname{arcsec}{\left (x \right )}`

The patch has been saved to `output/sympy__sympy-14774_preds.json`.
